user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Nod Publishers. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 08:46 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Nod Publishers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 09:19 EST
Subject: deployment blockers - Action Required

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 11:08 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Nod Publishers deployment blockers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-03-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Nod Publishers)

[00:01:15] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is deployment blockers.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] David (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:24:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:29:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:32:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:37:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:40:00] David (Nod Publishers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:43:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Nod Publishers: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
